overview: The Village of Weston Board of Trustees approved increased park and recreation impact fees for new residential development, passed multiple ordinances including mobile food vendor licensing and e-bike regulations, and held extensive discussion about the farmers market relocation to Kennedy Park. The board also adjourned the Board of Review to June 15, 2026.

agenda.item: Board of Review called to order and adjourned to June 15, 2026
Board of Trustees meeting called to order, roll call
Public hearing on park and recreation impact fees (Ordinance 26-010 and Resolution 2026-015)
Acknowledgment of reports from boards, committees, and commissions with chair updates
Consent agenda including vouchers and license renewals
Ordinances including mobile food vendor, nuisances, and e-bike regulations
Discussion on remote meeting attendance policy amendments

discussions.body: Staff presented a proposal to increase park impact fees from $300 to $761 for single family units, $250 to $563 for duplex units, and $250 to $495 for multifamily units, consistent with the 2022 public facilities needs assessment. Jim Pitel commented during public hearing that the village lacks real data on park maintenance costs. Trustee Motti supported the fees, noting population increases require more park space. Ordinance 26-010 passed unanimously with motion by Lope Sorrell, second by Zagami. Resolution 2026-015 passed unanimously with motion by Hermling, second by Clark.

Chief Finkey explained this creates a regional reciprocal inspection program with Marshfield, Wisconsin Rapids, Stevens Point, and other municipalities. When one department inspects a food truck, a sticker is placed allowing it to operate in all participating communities without additional inspections. Approved unanimously with motion by Lope, second by Zagami.

Significant debate occurred over policy language. Trustees Beck and others pushed to remove requirements that remote attendance be justified by health, safety, or welfare reasons, arguing chairs should not evaluate excuses. Trustees Hermling and Motti voted against the amendment. The board approved striking sentences requiring justification, keeping only requirements for 12-hour notice, camera use, and prohibition from closed sessions. Amendment passed with two no votes (Hermling, Motti), then original motion as amended passed with same two dissents.

discussions.item: Farmers Market Location at Kennedy Park
discussions.body: Discussion-only item addressed parking concerns at new Kennedy Park location after loss of Camp Phillips site. Trustee Beck raised safety concerns about cars parking on grass near vendors. Renee explained the market grew from 25 to 53 vendor spots. Mont Miller Park was discussed but rejected due to poor visibility and distance. Trustee Beck questioned costs; staff confirmed the market operates at break-even or profit through vendor fees and grants for food assistance programs. No action taken.

discussions.item: Engineering Tech Position and Outside Consultants
discussions.body: Public works director Michael explained the village has struggled to fill an engineering technician position due to limited workforce in central Wisconsin, noting even consulting firms lack local staff. Two contracts totaling approximately $108,000 for outside engineering oversight were on the agenda. Jim Pitel criticized during public comment that the administrator should be held accountable for not filling the position.

discussions.item: Secondhand Dealer License - Scoffield Coin and Hobby LLC
discussions.body: Approved with amendment requiring satisfactory background check completion and receipt of occupancy permit, as the business location at 5703 Jamar Street is still under construction. Motion by Hermling, second by Clark, amendment by Beck, passed unanimously.

publicComment: Jim Pitel of Deon 52 Street spoke during public comments, criticizing the $108,000 expenditure for outside engineering consultants when an engineering technician position remains unfilled. He questioned why Lisa Beck was introduced at the SAFER meeting before official appointment, calling it inappropriate government business outside the boardroom. He also criticized lack of information on Kennedy Park in the meeting packet and called for administrator accountability. Pitel also spoke during the park impact fee public hearing, arguing the village lacks real data on park maintenance costs.
actionItems: Park and recreation impact fees increased effective immediately per Ordinance 26-010 and Resolution 2026-015
Board of Review adjourned to June 15, 2026
Remote meeting attendance policy amended to remove justification requirements
Golden Mua and Stephanie Daniels appointed to CLIPS committee
Scoffield Coin and Hobby LLC secondhand dealer license approved pending background check and occupancy permit
Comprehensive plan steering committee first meeting scheduled for Monday, June 1st at 6:00 PM
Staff to bring Weston Avenue speed limit study back to board after construction completion
Committee and commission applications to be included in board packets going forward
Intergovernmental agreement to be drafted for assisting Town of Ringle with tornado damage building inspections
title: Board of Trustees
